description_long: This radiator features a tall, rectangular white panel with a prominent vertical grille section in the center, composed of evenly spaced yellow slats. The grille is slightly raised from the main body, supported by visible brackets, and likely serves as the primary heat-dissipating area. The main body appears to be made of metal, finished in a matte white, while the grille is a contrasting yellow, possibly powder-coated for durability. At the base of the grille, a small metallic plate or label is visible, suggesting a brand or model identifier. The sides of the radiator are flat and unadorned, emphasizing its slim profile, while the back is a solid, featureless panel. This design is typical for modern wall-mounted radiators found in urban interiors, providing both functional heating and a visually clean appearance.
description: A slim, rectangular white radiator with a central yellow vertical grille and a small metallic label at the base. The grille is raised and supported by brackets, giving the radiator a modern, clean look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full white panel, central yellow vertical grille, and a small metallic label at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile and the raised yellow grille supported by brackets.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid white panel with no visible features or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the protruding yellow grille.
attribute_pipe_connections: none visible; likely concealed or rear entry
